channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Trump to deliver prime-time address amid low approval and ongoing Iran conflict
item.description: US President Donald Trump is scheduled to deliver his first prime-time address regarding the ongoing conflict in Iran. The White House has not released specifics, but the speech comes shortly after Tr...

item.title: Gikomba trader laments loss after demolitions, recalls Raila Odinga’s concern for common citizens
item.description: A woman named Isabel Githinji has touched the hearts of many Kenyans after speaking emotionally about losing her stall during the recent Gikomba market demolitions. The government demolished sections ...

item.title: Trump’s War on Iran Deepens Tensions Between US and NATO Allies
item.description: The article reports on growing tensions between the United States and its NATO allies following President Donald Trump’s unilateral war on Iran. Many European countries have refused to back Washington...

item.title: Red House Emerges Winners at CSS Obete Ndoki 10th Inter-House Sports Competition
item.description: Last week for Obete, Ndoki, the Community Junior Secondary School don hold their 10th Annual Inter-House Sports Competition and e sweet well-well. Na Red House carry the trophy, while Green House foll...

item.title: UCLA study reveals 28% increase in student homelessness in L.A. County
item.description: A recent pair of research briefs from UCLA’s Center for the Transformation of Schools reports a 28% rise in student homelessness in Los Angeles County within a single school year, from 47,689 students...

item.title: APC Official Abdulsalami Ginsau Dies After Elevator Malfunction in Abuja Hotel
item.description: Barrister Abdulsalami Ginsau, a senior member of the All Progressives Congress (APC), has sadly passed away after getting trapped in a hotel elevator in Abuja on Tuesday. Witnesses talk say the elevat...

item.title: How to Save on UK Holidays with Parkdean Resorts in April 2026
item.description: Parkdean Resorts provides family-friendly holiday options at 66 locations across the UK, including coastal and countryside retreats. Accommodation choices range from caravans and lodges to glamping po...

item.title: Apple Issues Security Updates for iOS 18 to Protect Against DarkSword Exploit
item.description: Apple is taking an unusual step to release backported security patches for iOS 18 users in response to the spread of the DarkSword hacking tool. Traditionally, Apple has required users to update to th...

item.title: PM Modi reviews impact of Middle East conflict, directs steps to ensure supply stability and price control
item.description: On 1st April 2026, Prime Minister Narendra Modi chaired a special meeting of the Cabinet Committee on Security (CCS) to assess the ongoing conflict in the Middle East and its potential impact on India...

item.title: PDP Aspirant Gbenga Hashim Expresses Hope for Reconciliation within Party
item.description: Aspirant Gbenga Hashim has indicated that reconciliation is still attainable within the People's Democratic Party (PDP) despite recent internal divisions stemming from a factional convention. Hashim, ...

item.title: Finance Minister Champagne visits China to boost trade and attract investment amid global economic shifts
item.description: Canada’s Finance Minister, François-Philippe Champagne, is on a two-day visit to Beijing as part of Canada’s wider effort to diversify its trade relationships and strengthen the national economy. The ...

item.title: LTFRB adopts four-day work schedule under energy-saving directive
item.description: Starting April 6, 2026, the Land Transportation Franchising and Regulatory Board (LTFRB) will follow a four-day work week in line with the government’s new energy conservation policy. This move suppor...

item.title: Delhi Court Convicts MP MLA Rajendra Bharti for Cheating and Forgery of Bank Records
item.description: A Delhi court has convicted Madhya Pradesh Congress MLA Rajendra Bharti and former bank cashier Raghuvir Prajapati in a long-standing case of cheating and forgery involving the Zila Sahkari Krishi aur...

item.title: Key Trade Barriers Identified in U.S. Report on Foreign Markets
item.description: The U.S. recently released a report highlighting the top ten foreign trade barriers affecting American businesses. This report identifies countries and sectors where U.S. companies face the greatest c...

item.title: IndiGo revises ticket prices after aviation fuel cost increases
item.description: IndiGo, one of India’s leading airline carriers, has announced a revision in airfare prices following a sharp 25% increase in aviation turbine fuel (ATF) prices declared by the Ministry of Petroleum o...

item.title: Cameo integrates with TikTok to simplify celebrity video requests
item.description: Cameo, the celebrity greeting app, has launched a new integration with TikTok to help U.S. creators offer personalized video messages directly to their followers within the short-form video platform. ...

item.title: Six people lose lives in Dera Ismail Khan firing; police kill key suspect
item.description: In Dera Ismail Khan, at least six individuals were killed while two others sustained injuries in a firing incident in Mangal village of Prova tehsil, police officials reported on Wednesday. The attack...

item.title: Australia’s Teen Social Media Ban Shows Limited Effectiveness and Raises New Risks
item.description: The Australian government’s world-first ban on social media use by children under 16 has largely failed to achieve its intended goals. Despite the legislation, roughly seven in ten children continue t...

item.title: Candidates cleared for Tanzania Olympic Committee elections
item.description: The Tanzania Olympic Committee (TOC) is gearing up for its General Election on April 12 in Dar es Salaam. After a preliminary screening, 24 candidates were cleared to contest for different positions. ...

item.title: No Jackpot Winners for April 1 PCSO Lotto Draws
item.description: On April 1, 2026, the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) reported that no one won the jackpot prizes in its major lotto draws. The Megalotto 6/45 had a jackpot prize of P15,000,000.00, but t...
